title: First 1500: Worked / Didn't
description: Swipe between lanes and tap to swap colors as you steer a first-time dev's earnings through matching gates and risky Worked-or-Didn't choices on the way to $1500.

howToPlayJson.title: First 1500: Worked / Didn't
howToPlayJson.objective: Reach your first $1500 in a 2-4 minute run by matching your token color with incoming lane gates, then push into overtime for leaderboard score; each match triggers a cash chime, each combo step thickens the beat, and each mistake burns one

controls.outcome: Move your token to a safer or more profitable lane before the next gate lands
Flip your token between the two active colors to match the next gate

controls.input: Stay in the lane marked Worked or Didn't when the split arrives
controls.outcome: Worked boosts multiplier and speed; Didn't banks combo cash and soft-resets the pattern

steps: Start each run at $0 with three receipt hearts and a x1 multiplier.
Match your token color to incoming gates to earn cash; consecutive matches raise the combo multiplier.
Every few seconds, a split wave stamps one lane as WORKED and another as DIDN'T.
Passing through WORKED locks your current color for the next three gates, adds a multiplier boost, and makes the stream faster.
Passing through DIDN'T instantly banks your current combo as safe cash, rerolls upcoming colors, and slightly slows the stream.
A color mismatch, lane crash, or hitting a locked gate costs one receipt heart; lose all three and the run ends.
Reaching $1500 completes the 'first payout' and unlocks high-speed overtime where score keeps climbing for the leaderboard.
howToPlayJson.scoring: Each matched gate pays cash, combo raises the payout, WORKED choices stack a risk bonus, and DIDN'T choices convert live combo into banked money; leaderboard ranking is based on total cash earned, with $1500 as the core
tips: Use DIDN'T when your color lock would trap you in a bad lane pattern.
Chain short WORKED streaks early, then bank before the speed spike becomes unmanageable.
Listen for the beat change: faster percussion means the stream is accelerating with your multiplier.
Offline play stores local high scores immediately and can sync later without changing run rules.
